incorrects.description: Student selected 12, which does not match the 5s pattern and is too small

steps.text: 7 times 5 means 7 groups of 5. Skip-count by 5s seven times. Does that get you to 12?

incorrects.description: Student selected 30, which is 6 × 5, one group short

steps.text: 30 is close but check the first factor. You need 7 groups of 5, not 6. Skip-count by 5s one more time.

incorrects.description: Student selected 40, which is 8 × 5, one group too many

steps.text: You went too far. Count the groups carefully. You need 7 groups of 5, not 8. Which product comes before 40 in the 5s pattern?

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: Animation shows skip-counting by 5s seven times: 5, 10, 15, 20, 25, 30, 35. Each count highlights sequentially.

steps.text: Let me show you. 7 groups of 5. I skip-count by 5s: 5, 10, 15, 20, 25, 30, 35. Seven jumps lands on 35. The product is 35. It ends in 5, which matches the 5s pattern. 7 times 5 equals 35.
